cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question. The question is asking why Ms. Leibfried asked the author to recite a passage in front of the class. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to remember the details from the passage. The author was a shy student, the only nonwhite student, feeling out of place. She was into reading and writing, especially novels. Ms. Leibfried noticed her interest and even offered reading suggestions like "The Bell Jar." The big project was a book report, but Ms. Leibfried suggested the author do something "a little different." The author chose a passage from "The Bell Jar" to recite. After the recitation, the class applauded, and Ms. Leibfried praised her for picking a sensitive piece and delivering it beautifully. The author felt confident for the first time. Now, why did the teacher suggest this? Let's consider the options. Option A: Test her memory. But the passage doesn't mention anything about memory. It was about reciting a passage, which involves comprehension and expression, not just memorization. So probably not A. Option B: Make her earn respect. The author was shy and maybe didn't care what classmates thought before. The result was that the class applauded her, but the teacher's intention was likely to encourage her, not to make her earn respect. The teacher was supportive, so B might not be the main reason. Option C: Deepen her understanding of the book. The author chose a passage she considered the best, which might indicate analysis, but the teacher's suggestion was for the project. However, the immediate effect was about the author's confidence. The teacher noticed the interest and wanted to help her come out of her shell. Maybe not C. Option D: Guide her to build good relationships. The author was introverted, and by reciting in front of the class, she might have become more confident and thus able to connect better. The teacher's intention was to help her feel less shy and build confidence, which could lead to better relationships. The result was that the class applauded, but the teacher's action was aimed at supporting the author's growth. But let me check the text again. The author says Ms. Leibfried took time to make her feel less shy and offered reading suggestions. The project was a book report, and the teacher suggested a different approach. The author was nervous but after the recitation, she felt confident. So the main reason was to help her overcome shyness and build confidence, which relates to building relationships (D) or maybe helping her feel part of the class. But the options don't have "build confidence" directly. Option D is about building relationships. Alternatively, maybe C is about understanding the book better. Wait, the teacher said that the passage was particularly sensitive and delivered beautifully. The author chose it because she thought it was the best. So maybe the teacher wanted her to engage deeply with the text, thus deepening her understanding.
